summary: Live black-box pytest suite (22 tests) for the HushTV Canada $40 CAD/year Interac payment gateway against https://hushtv.xyz/. 22/22 PASSED. Covers health, order create + idempotency (reuse), case normalization, empty-username 400/422, order status (known + 404), public license lookup, admin auth (missing/wrong token → 401), grant 12-months (~365 days), grant extension (1-month additive), revoke, admin listings (orders + licenses), force IMAP poll (round-trip validates Gmail credentials), plus regression on /api/sports/home and /api/sync/health.

minor.issue: Could not exercise the actual pay-by-email branch black-box (would require sending a real Interac e-Transfer). Existing /app/sync_server/tests/test_canada_payment_api.py covers it locally via synthetic email bytes fed to _process_single_email(). Force-poll endpoint returned checked=59,matched=0,errors=0 — proves IMAP login + inbox scan + parser path all work; only the actual amount/Order-ID match could not be triggered.
Successful (paid) status response was not exercised end-to-end against the live gateway — would require a real Interac deposit. Admin-grant path (which uses the same canada_licenses row) IS verified.

action_items: (Optional, low priority) Consider returning a 200 with a clear error body for empty xtream_username on POST /api/canada/order/create — currently FastAPI/Pydantic returns 422 for missing key and the explicit `if not user: raise 400` returns 400 for empty-string. Both work; just inconsistent. Tests accept both.

(Code-review, low priority) start_poller() spawns a daemon Thread that sleeps 30 s between scans; if FastAPI shuts down mid-IMAP the connection is leaked. Acceptable for a long-running prod service; revisit if you ever do graceful shutdown.

(Code-review, low priority) /api/admin/canada/poll is not rate-limited. If exposed beyond ops it could be used to hammer Gmail's IMAP and risk a temporary ban. It currently requires the admin token so risk is low, but worth a per-token cooldown (e.g., 1 call / 10 s).

(Doc) The /order/create response shape varies — `already_licensed:true` (no `order` key) vs `reused:true` vs `reused:false`. Android client must handle all three. Suggest documenting in the OpenAPI schema.
critical_code_review_comments: Idempotency works correctly: duplicate POST /api/canada/order/create for the same xtream_username re-uses the same pending Order ID until the 60-minute TTL expires. Verified live (test_create_reuses_existing_pending_order).
Username case normalization is enforced server-side (.strip().lower()) on all three writer paths: order/create, admin/grant, admin/revoke. Order/status response echoes lowercase. Verified.

Grant is additive — second grant adds months ON TOP of existing expiry rather than resetting. Verified delta_ms ∈ (25d, 35d) for a 1-month top-up. Means an admin running `/grant months=12` twice gives a 24-month license, which is correct but worth flagging in admin docs.

Already-licensed users hitting /order/create get a short-circuit `already_licensed:true` response with the license blob and NO new Order ID — prevents needless Order ID inflation. Good design. Verified.
_check_admin returns 503 if ADMIN_TOKEN env var is unset on the server but 401 if token is wrong. Current deployment has the token set (verified — 401 is what we get for wrong token, not 503). Good.

IMAP scan looks back 7 days and de-dupes by UID via canada_processed_emails table → safe against duplicate credits even if the poller crashes and reprocesses old mail. Verified that 59 emails were checked with 0 errors and 0 (re)matches on a re-poll.
Order ID generator uses random.randint with collision retry — re-check needed only at scale (8-digit space = 100M, plenty of headroom for 40 CAD/year customers).

SQLite WAL + isolation_level=None + RLock — fine for a single-process FastAPI server. If they ever scale horizontally this becomes a hard limit; document that canada_payment_module is single-instance only.
test_credentials: Admin token X-Admin-Token: b4010e5b9271baa489f3cbaabb2baf58f96c814146054a7f. All public canada endpoints require no auth. No user/password creds in scope for this module.
seed_data_creation: Each test run creates ~7 throwaway xtream_usernames of the form qa_<tag>_<unix_ts>_<pid>. Pending orders auto-expire after 60 minutes. Licenses granted to qa_* users during the run are revoked by a session-scoped autouse fixture (_qa_cleanup) so the canada_licenses table stays clean. Verified by running the suite twice — no leftover qa_* licenses.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/backend/tests/test_canada_payment_live.py as the live regression suite for the Canada Interac gateway. It is fully self-contained (no MongoDB, no fixtures beyond a requests.Session) and targets https://hushtv.xyz directly. To re-run: `python -m pytest /app/backend/tests/test_canada_payment_live.py -v`. The session cleanup fixture revokes all qa_* licenses on exit so it is safe to re-run repeatedly. The only branches NOT covered live are (a) successful paid email reconciliation (requires real Interac e-Transfer) and (b) order/status with status=paid via real email — both ARE covered by /app/sync_server/tests/test_canada_payment_api.py via synthetic email bytes. IMAP force-poll confirmed Gmail auth + scanning works in production: checked=59,matched=0,errors=0.
rca of the issue: No live bug. All 22 tests green on first run. The only black-box gap is real-Interac email arrival, which is provably mocked-locally by test_canada_payment_api.py::test_license_unpaid_then_paid_via_email_match and confirmed-alive in prod by the force-poll returning errors=0 against 59 recent inbox messages — i.e. Gmail IMAP auth + parser pipeline are healthy; the only thing that hasn't fired in this run is an actual amount/Order-ID match.
